name: Babel Language-Collapse Source Packet
purpose: Selected public-domain anchors for a Revelation room where a modern command-language system becomes a tower-making anomaly. The room should treat centralized speech, name-making, construction, and scattering as active mechanics.

fragments.source_circumstance: The people have one language, settle in Shinar, make bricks and tar, build a city and tower, and say they will make themselves a name so they will not be scattered.
fragments.source_excerpt: The whole earth had one language. They made brick and tar, built a city and tower, and sought to make themselves a name.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: An Institute emergency-command platform forces all radio orders into one approved syntax. The words begin making walls, ramps, and evacuation routes obey a single name.
fragments.escalation_thread: First radio orders translate badly. Then spoken commands produce physical construction. Finally every attempt to coordinate raises a tower-like stack of doors, scaffolds, and bodies.
generation_rules: Name the sin: centralizing command language to make institutional authority unchallengeable.
Make construction materials visible: brick, tar, concrete, wet mortar, scaffolds, lift doors, or stacked forms.
Closure must scatter, localize, or humble the command language rather than simply power off a machine.

fragments.source_circumstance: Language is confused, the builders no longer understand one another, the city stops, and the people are scattered from Babel.
fragments.source_excerpt: Their speech was confused so they could not understand one another. They stopped building, and were scattered from Babel.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: The solution can require deliberate decentralization: independent hand signals, handwritten local authority, separate evacuation cells, and refusal to let one command phrase rule the site.
fragments.escalation_thread: If the team tries one more perfect command, the anomaly climbs. If they accept partial speech and distributed command, the structure loses coherence.
generation_rules: Let the follow-up reveal what one-language policy was hiding.
Do not make the room a generic translator glitch; tie speech to institutional pride and construction.
The revelation should be a concrete moral diagnosis, not a vague statement about language.

fragments.source_title: ATP 3-21.8 Infantry Rifle Platoon and Squad
fragments.source_circumstance: Patrols need clear task and purpose, security, navigation, danger-area crossing, reconnaissance, detailed searches, and handling of casualties and detainees.
fragments.source_excerpt: A patrol has a clear task and purpose, secures itself, searches, deals with casualties and detainees, then reports actions, observations, and condition.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: The squad must split command into clear local tasks: secure civilians, mark safe language zones, search dispatch terminals, and report who still understands whom.
fragments.escalation_thread: Over-centralized orders fail. Small cells with clear task and purpose can still move under Babel pressure.
generation_rules: Use task/purpose, corridor security, searches, casualties, and report-backs as procedural texture.
Give one character a plan that works only if their own communications hold.
If a plan fails, change that character's stress or mental state and worsen the command environment.

fragments.source_title: Judgment and Decision Making Under Stress: An Overview for Emergency Managers
fragments.source_circumstance: Emergency warnings are often unclear, equipment may fail, leadership gaps create confusion, and time pressure narrows attention.
fragments.source_excerpt: Warnings can be unclear, equipment may fail, people respond to leadership, and time pressure narrows attention to critical cues.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: The squad cannot trust every radio, screen, or translated order. Character plans should read as professional decisions under incomplete information.
fragments.escalation_thread: A failed plan turns coordination into more pressure: wrong doors open, civilians follow bad instructions, or one officer stops trusting their own voice.
generation_rules: Do not expose numeric odds or reward labels.
Every choice must be a named character's plan with a clear intended action.
Backfires should affect the proposing character when appropriate.
